findings.evidence: grep hits: lines 71-87 expose `sum_const_mul`/`sum_real_const_mul`; lines 104-138 provide origin lemmas only for `w i • F i x`.
findings.fix: Either drop the public `*_const_mul` variants or add matching `sum_const_mul_apply_zero_*` and `sum_real_const_mul_apply_zero_*` lemmas.
findings.issue: `sum_const_mul` and `sum_real_const_mul` are public weighted-mixture forms, but the origin API only characterizes the scalar-notation forms, so users of the exposed `*` variants must unfold through `Algebra.smul_def` to prove normalization.

findings.evidence: theorem declarations at lines 104, 113, 121, and 134 have no `@[simp]` attribute.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the normal-form origin lemmas, at least `sum_smul_apply_zero_of_apply_zero_eq_one`, `sum_smul_apply_zero_eq_one`, `sum_real_smul_apply_zero_of_apply_zero_eq_one`, and `sum_real_smul_apply_zero_eq_one`, provided the conditional rewrites do not loop.
findings.issue: The origin-value lemmas are the normal-form API for normalized mixtures but are not marked `@[simp]`, so simplification will not use them automatically at the intended normalization point.

summary: The closure surface is mostly targeted, but the exposed weighted-sum API is missing matching normal-form support for the multiplication variants and its origin normal forms are not annotated for automation.
